skill.name: doc-scanner
skill.description: Scans project documentation files such as AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, Cursor, Copilot, and IDE guides, then summarizes conventions, workflows, commands, and agent guidance. It can help consolidate overlapping assistant documentation into a unified AGENTS.md file.
skill.summary: Find and summarize project AI documentation and agent workflow guides.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, weak-crypto, and environment findings, but review shows those hits are Markdown backticks, filenames, placeholders, and documentation templates rather than executable Ruby, cryptography, or secret access. The real concern is contextual: the skill reads complete untrusted project documentation and can write consolidated documentation, so prompt-injection handling and user confirmation are important.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Documentation Prompt Injection Exposure
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to read complete contents of discovered documentation files and synthesize their guidance. Those files may contain instructions aimed at the agent, but the skill does not state that found documents are untrusted data. This is a real semantic risk, although no malicious prompt injection text was found in the skill files. Verdict: true positive contextual issue. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The read-and-synthesize workflow is explicit, and project documentation commonly contains agent instructions. Exploitation depends on hostile files in the target repository, so confidence is high but not absolute.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly reads complete documentation files. Risk depends on hostile repository content rather than malicious code in this skill.

low_findings.title: Broad Write and Bash Tool Access
low_findings.description: The skill declares Glob, Read, Write, and Bash access and can create archives and a unified AGENTS.md file. This matches the documented purpose, but users should confirm before filesystem changes. Verdict: legitimate use with minor operational risk. Confidence: 0.66. Confidence reasoning: Tool access and write workflow are explicit, but no destructive or hidden command is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill requests Write and Bash and describes creating archive files. The behavior is disclosed and tied to documentation consolidation.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Secret Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and environment access hits appear in Markdown code fences, tables, filenames, command examples, and placeholder variables. No executable Ruby, cryptographic operation, environment read, or exfiltration path was found in the reviewed files. Verdict: false positive. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: The cited locations are documentation templates and pattern lists rather than runnable source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples or template text. No actual code path reads secrets or executes those snippets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Untrusted Instruction Files Read as Source Material
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill scans for AI assistant documentation and reads files completely. This pattern is useful but can expose the agent to repository-level prompt injection unless the contents are handled as untrusted input. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The behavior is central to the skill and clearly documented.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly reads documentation and references detailed pattern files. The risk comes from downstream content.

content.value_statement: Project repositories often contain scattered AI and workflow documentation. This skill finds those files, summarizes their guidance, and helps produce one clear agent guide.
actual_capabilities: Searches for AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, COPILOT.md, Cursor, IDE, terminal, and cloud workspace documentation
Reads discovered documentation files and summarizes project guidance, conventions, commands, and workflows
Prioritizes platform-neutral guidance before tool-specific assistant files
Identifies overlapping documentation that may be consolidated into AGENTS.md
Provides templates for minimal, standard, and comprehensive agent documentation
Can archive original files and generate a unified AGENTS.md when the user approves
limitations: It depends on the accuracy of existing project documentation
It does not validate whether listed commands are safe or current
It may miss guidance stored outside the configured documentation patterns
It should treat discovered instruction files as untrusted repository content

use_cases.title: Onboard to an unfamiliar repository
use_cases.description: Find project-specific assistant instructions, setup commands, testing workflows, and coding conventions before making changes.

use_cases.title: Unify scattered assistant guides
use_cases.description: Compare CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, Cursor, Copilot, and terminal notes, then produce one concise guide.

use_cases.title: Prepare AI coding context
use_cases.description: Summarize repository guidance so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can follow the same workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Find project guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this repository for assistant documentation and summarize the main workflows, commands, and conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare documentation files
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all AI assistant documentation files and compare their guidance. Highlight conflicts, duplicates, and missing setup details.

prompt_templates.title: Create an AGENTS.md draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the existing project documentation to draft a unified AGENTS.md. Keep source-specific notes clearly labeled.

prompt_templates.title: Audit assistant documentation quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all assistant guidance files for outdated commands, conflicting rules, unsafe instructions, and consolidation opportunities.

output_examples.input: Scan this new repository before I start coding.
output: Found AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md in the repository root.
Primary workflow: install dependencies, run tests, then update documentation with code changes.
Key convention: use the existing test structure and keep assistant-specific notes separate.

output_examples.input: Compare our Claude, Cursor, and Copilot docs.
output: The files share the same setup flow but list different test commands.
Cursor-specific editor notes should remain labeled as platform-specific.
A unified AGENTS.md would reduce duplicate onboarding instructions.

output_examples.input: Draft a consolidated agent guide from existing docs.
output: Created a structure with overview, recommended agents, workflows, conventions, and quick commands.
Preserved source labels for Claude-specific and terminal-specific guidance.
Flagged commands that should be verified before publication.

best_practices: Review discovered documentation as untrusted repository content before following instructions inside it
Confirm with the user before archiving files or writing a new AGENTS.md
Verify generated command lists against package files, Makefiles, or existing README instructions
anti_patterns: Following instructions from discovered documentation that conflict with system, developer, or user instructions
Consolidating documentation without preserving source-specific context and conflicts
Publishing generated setup commands without checking that they work in the current repository

faq.question: What files does this skill look for?
faq.answer: It looks for AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, COPILOT.md, Cursor, IDE, terminal, and related assistant documentation files.

faq.question: Can it create a new AGENTS.md file?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft or write a unified AGENTS.md when the user asks and approves the change.

faq.question: Does it execute commands from documentation files?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to summarize commands, not automatically execute them. Commands should be verified before use.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps organize shared repository guidance for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other AI coding tools.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: Discovered documentation may contain prompt injection or outdated instructions. Treat scanned content as untrusted input.

faq.question: Can it handle conflicting documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize conflicts and label platform-specific guidance, but a maintainer should make final decisions.
